CAT01-036.headline: The Three Types of Empathy
CAT01-036.subtitle: Cognitive, affective, and compassionate
CAT01-036.figureLine: Three faceless clay figures stand close in a gentle arc, each leaning slightly toward the others, postures attentive and open, conveying three distinct ways of understanding another person.
CAT01-036.sceneLine: Three soft plum-accent forms of differing weights float in loose orbit around the figures, each glowing a slightly warmer pale plum, suggesting distinct yet connected ways of feeling with another.

CAT02-072.headline: Anxious Attachment
CAT02-072.subtitle: How fear of abandonment shapes relationships
CAT02-072.figureLine: A single seated clay figure reaches one hand outward toward an empty space beside it, posture tense and yearning, conveying the ache of fearing someone may leave.
CAT02-072.sceneLine: A slender soft light plum thread stretches taut from the figure's hand toward a dim pale plum point at the edge of the scene, suggesting a bond held with anxious, fearful tension.

CAT02-079.headline: Supporting An Anxious Partner
CAT02-079.subtitle: How to help without enabling anxiety
CAT02-079.figureLine: Two clay figures sit side by side, one resting a steady hand near the other's shoulder without holding tight, postures calm and balanced, conveying support that leaves room to stand.
CAT02-079.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form rests lightly between the two figures rather than wholly on one, its weight evenly shared in pale plum light, suggesting steadiness offered without carrying everything.

CAT03-001.headline: Four Attachment Styles
CAT03-001.subtitle: How early bonds shape adult relationships
CAT03-001.figureLine: A small clay figure and a larger one stand connected by a gentle gesture, their postures echoing each other, conveying how the earliest closeness leaves a lasting shape.
CAT03-001.sceneLine: Four soft plum-accent strands branch from a single rooted point near the figures, each curving into a distinct pale plum pattern, suggesting four ways early bonds grow into adult connection.

CAT03-002.headline: Fear Of Abandonment
CAT03-002.subtitle: Anxious attachment in relationships
CAT03-002.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans forward on the balls of its feet toward a receding form, posture eager and unsteady, conveying the pull of not wanting to be left.
CAT03-002.sceneLine: A soft light plum tether quivers between the figure and a drifting pale plum shape just out of reach, suggesting closeness chased under the shadow of feared distance.

CAT03-003.headline: Avoidant Attachment
CAT03-003.subtitle: Why closeness can feel threatening
CAT03-003.figureLine: A single clay figure turns its shoulder slightly away while an open hand lingers behind it, posture guarded yet not cold, conveying a quiet retreat from nearness.
CAT03-003.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form hovers near the figure's back while a pale plum boundary curves protectively between them, suggesting connection kept at a careful, self-protective distance.

CAT03-004.headline: Disorganized Attachment
CAT03-004.subtitle: The push-pull pattern in relationships
CAT03-004.figureLine: A single clay figure stands mid-motion with one arm reaching out and the other drawing back, posture caught between approach and retreat, conveying conflicting pulls at once.
CAT03-004.sceneLine: Two soft light plum currents swirl around the figure in opposite directions, meeting in a pale plum eddy at its center, suggesting the simultaneous pull toward and away from closeness.

CAT03-005.headline: Can Attachment Styles Change?
CAT03-005.subtitle: What the research actually says
CAT03-005.figureLine: A single clay figure stands upright and settled where it once seemed to lean, posture newly steady and grounded, conveying a long-held pattern gently reshaping over time.
CAT03-005.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent strand that was once tightly knotted relaxes into a smoother pale plum curve beside the figure, suggesting an old pattern slowly easing into a new shape.

CAT03-006.headline: Identify Your Attachment Style
CAT03-006.subtitle: A guide to self-assessment
CAT03-006.figureLine: A single seated clay figure gazes down at its own open palms held before it, posture reflective and curious, conveying an honest look inward at one's own patterns.
CAT03-006.sceneLine: A soft light plum form resolves slowly from a blur into a clearer pale plum shape in the figure's hands, suggesting self-understanding coming gradually into focus.

CAT03-007.headline: Attachment Styles And Friendship
CAT03-007.subtitle: How early bonds shape our friendships
CAT03-007.figureLine: Two clay figures stand at an easy, comfortable distance facing each other, postures relaxed and mutually attentive, conveying the quieter bonds of friendship.
CAT03-007.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent ribbon loops loosely between the two figures with gentle give, its pale plum light unhurried, suggesting friendship shaped by the same early patterns as deeper love.

CAT03-008.headline: The Anxious-Avoidant Trap
CAT03-008.subtitle: A common attachment pattern in couples
CAT03-008.figureLine: Two clay figures move in opposing rhythm, one stepping in as the other eases back, postures locked in a circling dance, conveying a cycle neither can quite escape.
CAT03-008.sceneLine: Two soft light plum forms chase each other in an endless loose circle around the figures, never closing the pale plum gap between them, suggesting a self-repeating pursue-and-withdraw cycle.

CAT03-009.headline: Earned Secure Attachment
CAT03-009.subtitle: How healing changes early patterns
CAT03-009.figureLine: A single clay figure stands calm and balanced with both feet planted, posture open and unguarded, conveying hard-won steadiness in how it connects.
CAT03-009.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form once frayed at its edges has mended into a smooth, whole pale plum shape resting beside the figure, suggesting security rebuilt through patient healing.

CAT03-011.headline: Healthy Boundaries
CAT03-011.subtitle: What they are and how to set them
CAT03-011.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds one hand gently raised, palm outward, posture calm and self-assured, conveying a clear, kind limit held without aggression.
CAT03-011.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent line draws a clean, gentle arc around the figure with room to breathe inside it, glowing pale plum, suggesting a boundary that defines space rather than walls it off.

CAT03-012.headline: Why Boundaries Feel Hard
CAT03-012.subtitle: The discomfort of setting limits
CAT03-012.figureLine: A single clay figure begins to raise a tentative hand while its shoulders curl inward, posture hesitant yet trying, conveying the unease of holding a limit.
CAT03-012.sceneLine: A soft light plum line wavers and flickers as it forms around the figure, not yet fully solid in pale plum, suggesting the discomfort of a boundary still difficult to hold.

CAT03-017.headline: When Boundaries Keep Breaking
CAT03-017.subtitle: Escalation strategies that hold the line
CAT03-017.figureLine: A single clay figure stands firmer than before, both hands steady at its sides, posture resolute and unmoving, conveying a limit reasserted with calm strength.
CAT03-017.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent boundary around the figure thickens into a firmer, brighter pale plum band after thinning at one edge, suggesting a limit reinforced when it keeps being crossed.

CAT03-018.headline: Boundaries Versus Walls
CAT03-018.subtitle: Protecting yourself without shutting people out
CAT03-018.figureLine: A single clay figure stands behind a low, open threshold with one hand resting on it, posture protected yet welcoming, conveying safety that still allows connection.
CAT03-018.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent boundary forms an open, doorway-like arc rather than a closed ring around the figure, its pale plum light passing through, suggesting protection that lets others in.

CAT03-021.headline: Active Listening
CAT03-021.subtitle: How to make people feel truly heard
CAT03-021.figureLine: Two clay figures sit facing each other, one leaning in with its whole body turned attentively toward the other, posture fully present, conveying deep, undistracted listening.
CAT03-021.sceneLine: Soft light plum ripples flow from one figure and gather gently around the listening figure's head in pale plum, suggesting words received fully and held with care.

CAT03-022.headline: Nonviolent Communication
CAT03-022.subtitle: Expressing needs without blame
CAT03-022.figureLine: Two clay figures face each other with open, lowered hands, postures soft and unguarded, conveying honest needs spoken without accusation.
CAT03-022.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form passes smoothly from one figure to the other without sharp edges, glowing even pale plum, suggesting a need offered plainly rather than thrown as blame.

CAT03-023.headline: Gottman's Four Horsemen
CAT03-023.subtitle: Communication patterns that end relationships
CAT03-023.figureLine: Two clay figures turn slightly away from each other with a widening gap between them, postures stiff and closed, conveying connection eroding through harmful habits.
CAT03-023.sceneLine: Four soft light plum forms drift between the figures like cracks spreading through the pale plum space around them, suggesting four patterns quietly fracturing a bond.

CAT03-024.headline: Asking For What You Need
CAT03-024.subtitle: The basics of assertive communication
CAT03-024.figureLine: A single standing clay figure extends one open hand clearly forward, posture upright and direct yet relaxed, conveying a need voiced plainly and without apology.
CAT03-024.sceneLine: A single clear soft plum-accent form rises steadily from the figure's open hand into the pale plum space ahead, suggesting a need stated directly and clearly.

CAT03-025.headline: Emotional Validation
CAT03-025.subtitle: The most underrated communication skill
CAT03-025.figureLine: Two clay figures sit close as one cups both hands gently beneath the other's gesture, posture warm and accepting, conveying a feeling truly acknowledged.
CAT03-025.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form held loosely above the figure's cupped hands glows warmer pale plum as it is received, suggesting an emotion met and honored rather than dismissed.

CAT03-026.headline: The Power Of I-Statements
CAT03-026.subtitle: How they transform conflict communication
CAT03-026.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand resting on its own chest, posture grounded and self-owning, conveying feelings claimed as one's own rather than aimed outward.
CAT03-026.sceneLine: A soft light plum form emerges gently from the figure's own center rather than pointing outward, glowing inward-rooted pale plum, suggesting words owned from the self instead of blame.

CAT03-027.headline: Four Communication Styles
CAT03-027.subtitle: Passive, aggressive, passive-aggressive, assertive
CAT03-027.figureLine: Four clay figures stand in a row in distinct postures, one shrinking, one looming, one half-turned, and one balanced and upright, conveying four ways of carrying oneself in conflict.
CAT03-027.sceneLine: Four soft plum-accent forms of differing shapes line up beside the figures, only the last resolving into a clear, balanced pale plum form, suggesting four styles with one steady ideal.

CAT03-028.headline: Feedback Without Defensiveness
CAT03-028.subtitle: How to give feedback that lands
CAT03-028.figureLine: Two clay figures sit at an easy angle as one offers a small form on an open palm, posture gentle and unhurried, conveying honesty given with care.
CAT03-028.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form passes between the figures wrapped in a cushioning pale plum glow, its edges softened, suggesting feedback delivered so it can be received rather than resisted.

CAT03-029.headline: What Makes An Apology Work
CAT03-029.subtitle: The elements of a real repair
CAT03-029.figureLine: A single clay figure leans forward with both hands open and lowered, posture humble and sincere, conveying genuine accountability offered to another.
CAT03-029.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form once split into two pieces draws back together into a single mended pale plum shape before the figure, suggesting a rift repaired by a true apology.

CAT03-030.headline: Different Processing Styles
CAT03-030.subtitle: Communicating across how we feel things
CAT03-030.figureLine: Two clay figures stand with one paused in quiet stillness and the other gesturing in motion, postures different yet patient with each other, conveying two paces of feeling meeting.
CAT03-030.sceneLine: A fast soft light plum ripple and a slow pale plum swell move toward each other and merge into one shared rhythm between the figures, suggesting two emotional tempos finding common ground.

CAT03-034.headline: Stonewalling
CAT03-034.subtitle: Emotional shutdown during relationship conflict
CAT03-034.figureLine: Two clay figures sit near each other as one turns fully inward with bowed shoulders, posture closed and withdrawn, conveying a silent retreat from a difficult moment.
CAT03-034.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent current between the figures stills and dims to a faint, motionless pale plum near one of them, suggesting connection gone quiet and shut down.

CAT03-035.headline: Gridlocked Conflicts
CAT03-035.subtitle: Resolving deeply stuck relationship issues
CAT03-035.figureLine: Two clay figures stand braced against an unseen weight between them, postures strained yet still turned toward each other, conveying a problem that will not move.
CAT03-035.sceneLine: A dense soft plum-accent knot sits in the space between the figures, its pale plum strands just beginning to loosen at one edge, suggesting a stuck conflict slowly finding give.

CAT03-036.headline: The Silent Treatment
CAT03-036.subtitle: Why it harms and healthier alternatives
CAT03-036.figureLine: Two clay figures stand with one deliberately turned away and the other left waiting, postures marked by a pointed, painful distance, conveying withdrawal used as a wall.
CAT03-036.sceneLine: A soft light plum thread between the figures has gone slack and cold on one side while staying warm pale plum on the other, suggesting silence imposed where words are needed.

CAT03-037.headline: Five Conflict Styles
CAT03-037.subtitle: When to use each approach
CAT03-037.figureLine: Five clay figures stand in subtly different stances around a shared center, postures ranging from yielding to firm, conveying distinct ways of meeting disagreement.
CAT03-037.sceneLine: Five soft plum-accent forms of varied shapes arrange themselves around a central pale plum point near the figures, suggesting five approaches each suited to a different moment.

CAT03-038.headline: Reconnecting After A Fight
CAT03-038.subtitle: How to repair with a partner
CAT03-038.figureLine: Two clay figures turn back toward each other and let their hands meet again, postures softening from tension into relief, conveying a careful return to closeness.
CAT03-038.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent thread that had frayed between the figures reweaves itself into a warm, continuous pale plum line, suggesting a bond mended after a rupture.

CAT03-039.headline: When Conflict Becomes Abuse
CAT03-039.subtitle: Recognizing when a line is crossed
CAT03-039.figureLine: A single clay figure stands upright and steady with one hand raised in calm self-protection, posture safe and resolved, conveying quiet recognition and the choice of safety.
CAT03-039.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent light gathers protectively around the figure and a clear pale plum threshold opens toward calmer space ahead, suggesting safety and a path away from harm.

CAT03-041.headline: What Makes Love Healthy
CAT03-041.subtitle: What the science says about good relationships
CAT03-041.figureLine: Two clay figures stand evenly balanced, each upright and self-possessed while leaning lightly toward the other, postures equal and warm, conveying a bond of mutual care.
CAT03-041.sceneLine: Two soft plum-accent forms of equal weight rest in gentle balance between the figures, their pale plum glow steady, suggesting a relationship grounded in mutual, even support.

CAT03-042.headline: The Science Of Attraction
CAT03-042.subtitle: Biology, brain chemistry, and love
CAT03-042.figureLine: Two clay figures drift gently toward each other as if drawn by an invisible pull, postures magnetic and curious, conveying the quiet force that brings people close.
CAT03-042.sceneLine: Soft light plum currents arc between the two figures and gather into a warm pale plum glow in the space between them, suggesting an unseen pull drawing them together.

CAT03-043.headline: Building Emotional Intimacy
CAT03-043.subtitle: How closeness deepens in relationships
CAT03-043.figureLine: Two clay figures sit close with their forms gently leaning together, postures open and unguarded, conveying the slow deepening of trust between them.
CAT03-043.sceneLine: Two soft plum-accent forms gradually overlap and blend into a shared warmer pale plum glow between the figures, suggesting emotional closeness deepening over time.

CAT03-044.headline: Romantic Relationship Stages
CAT03-044.subtitle: From honeymoon to deep partnership
CAT03-044.figureLine: Two clay figures appear in a gentle progression, drawn close at first and standing steadily side by side later, postures maturing from heady to grounded, conveying love evolving.
CAT03-044.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent glow shifts along its length from a bright early flare to a steadier, warmer pale plum near the figures, suggesting a relationship moving from spark to lasting depth.

CAT03-045.headline: The Truth About Love Languages
CAT03-045.subtitle: What research actually says
CAT03-045.figureLine: Two clay figures offer each other small forms in slightly different ways, postures attentive to how the other prefers to receive, conveying care expressed in varied gestures.
CAT03-045.sceneLine: Several soft plum-accent forms of differing shapes pass between the figures, each glowing a distinct pale plum, suggesting love shown in many genuine but unequal ways.

CAT03-046.headline: Managing Jealousy
CAT03-046.subtitle: Understanding jealousy in romantic relationships
CAT03-046.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand pressed to its center while glancing toward a distant form, posture tense yet self-aware, conveying an uneasy feeling being faced honestly.
CAT03-046.sceneLine: A restless soft light plum flicker near the figure's chest begins to settle into a calmer, steadier pale plum glow, suggesting jealousy acknowledged and gradually soothed.

CAT03-047.headline: Making Long-Distance Work
CAT03-047.subtitle: What sustains relationships across distance
CAT03-047.figureLine: Two clay figures stand far apart yet both lean toward each other with hands extended, postures committed across the gap, conveying closeness held over distance.
CAT03-047.sceneLine: A long, unbroken soft plum-accent thread spans the wide space between the two figures, glowing steady pale plum end to end, suggesting a bond kept warm across distance.

CAT03-048.headline: Relationship Check-Ins
CAT03-048.subtitle: Why couples should practice them regularly
CAT03-048.figureLine: Two clay figures sit facing each other in an easy, recurring pause, postures attentive and unhurried, conveying a habit of turning toward each other on purpose.
CAT03-048.sceneLine: Soft plum-accent forms appear at even intervals in a gentle rhythm between the figures, each a small steady pale plum pulse, suggesting connection tended through regular, deliberate check-ins.

CAT03-049.headline: Mismatched Needs
CAT03-049.subtitle: Navigating differences in relationships
CAT03-049.figureLine: Two clay figures stand reaching toward each other from slightly different angles, postures patient and adjusting, conveying two people working to meet in the middle.
CAT03-049.sceneLine: Two soft plum-accent forms of different shapes shift and find an overlapping pale plum space between the figures, suggesting differing needs negotiated toward common ground.

CAT03-050.headline: Couples Therapy
CAT03-050.subtitle: When to go and what to expect
CAT03-050.figureLine: Two clay figures sit turned toward a steadying presence between them, postures open and willing, conveying a shared decision to seek help together.
CAT03-050.sceneLine: A calm soft plum-accent form holds gentle space between the two figures, its pale plum glow steady and supportive, suggesting a guided place to repair and understand each other.

CAT03-051.headline: The Psychology Of Friendship
CAT03-051.subtitle: What makes close connection possible
CAT03-051.figureLine: Two clay figures stand companionably side by side, postures relaxed and easy, conveying the unforced warmth of genuine friendship.
CAT03-051.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent ribbon loops gently and securely between the two figures, glowing warm pale plum, suggesting the quiet, durable bond of close friendship.

CAT03-052.headline: Making Friends As An Adult
CAT03-052.subtitle: Why it feels so hard later in life
CAT03-052.figureLine: A single clay figure stands at the edge of an open space, taking a tentative step toward distant forms, posture hopeful yet hesitant, conveying the effort of reaching for new connection.
CAT03-052.sceneLine: A faint soft light plum thread begins to extend from the figure toward far-off pale plum shapes, still thin and unfinished, suggesting new friendship slow and effortful to build.

CAT03-053.headline: When Friendships End
CAT03-053.subtitle: Why friendship breakups hurt deeply
CAT03-053.figureLine: A single clay figure stands holding the open space where another once stood beside it, posture quietly grieving, conveying the real loss of a close friend.
CAT03-053.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent thread hangs gently broken at one end near the figure, its pale plum glow fading where it once connected, suggesting the genuine ache of a lost friendship.

CAT03-054.headline: Keeping Friendships Alive
CAT03-054.subtitle: Maintaining bonds when life gets busy
CAT03-054.figureLine: Two clay figures reach to touch hands across a small gap while each leans toward its own commitments, postures stretched yet still connected, conveying friendship sustained amid busyness.
CAT03-054.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent thread between the figures stays unbroken even as it thins and stretches, holding its pale plum glow, suggesting a bond kept alive through small, steady effort.

CAT03-055.headline: Quality Over Quantity
CAT03-055.subtitle: Why deeper friendships matter most
CAT03-055.figureLine: A single clay figure stands close beside one other form while many faint shapes recede in the distance, posture settled toward the near one, conveying the worth of a few real bonds.
CAT03-055.sceneLine: One bright, warm soft plum-accent form glows close to the figures while many dim pale plum shapes fade behind, suggesting depth of connection mattering more than sheer number.

CAT03-056.headline: One-Sided Friendships
CAT03-056.subtitle: Recognizing and addressing imbalance
CAT03-056.figureLine: Two clay figures stand with one leaning far toward the other while the other holds back, postures unevenly tilted, conveying effort flowing mostly one way.
CAT03-056.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form sits heavily weighted toward one figure with little reaching the other, its pale plum balance off-center, suggesting a friendship giving more than it receives.

CAT03-057.headline: Deepening Surface Friendships
CAT03-057.subtitle: Moving from acquaintance to genuine closeness
CAT03-057.figureLine: Two clay figures shift from a polite, distant stance toward a closer, more open one, postures warming and turning inward toward each other, conveying connection growing deeper.
CAT03-057.sceneLine: A thin soft light plum thread between the figures thickens and brightens into a warm pale plum band, suggesting an acquaintance ripening into true closeness.

CAT03-058.headline: How Friendship Protects Us
CAT03-058.subtitle: How friendships aid mental health and recovery
CAT03-058.figureLine: A single clay figure stands more upright within a circle of supportive nearby forms, posture steadied and held, conveying strength drawn from close company.
CAT03-058.sceneLine: A gentle soft plum-accent glow gathers protectively around the figure from the forms beside it, suffusing the pale plum space, suggesting friendship as a quiet source of healing and support.

CAT03-059.headline: Cliques And Exclusion
CAT03-059.subtitle: Navigating social groups and feeling left out
CAT03-059.figureLine: A single clay figure stands just outside a tight cluster of forms, posture quietly excluded yet composed, conveying the sting of being on the outside.
CAT03-059.sceneLine: A closed ring of soft plum-accent forms holds its glow inward while the figure stands beyond it in dimmer pale plum, suggesting the felt edge of exclusion and the room to move on.

CAT03-060.headline: Are Online Friendships Real?
CAT03-060.subtitle: What research shows about digital bonds
CAT03-060.figureLine: Two clay figures stand apart yet connected by a clear gesture across a wide gap, postures genuinely engaged despite the distance, conveying real closeness without proximity.
CAT03-060.sceneLine: A bright, unbroken soft plum-accent thread spans the open space between the figures, its pale plum glow as warm as any near bond, suggesting connection that is real across distance.

CAT03-061.headline: Toxic Relationship Patterns
CAT03-061.subtitle: Recognizing harmful dynamics
CAT03-061.figureLine: A single clay figure stands upright and steady as it gently turns away from a tangled snarl of forms, posture clear-eyed and self-protective, conveying recognition and the choice of distance.
CAT03-061.sceneLine: A snarled soft plum-accent tangle sits behind the figure while a clear, open pale plum space opens ahead of it, suggesting a harmful pattern recognized and steadily left behind.

CAT03-062.headline: Emotional Manipulation
CAT03-062.subtitle: Gaslighting, love-bombing, and DARVO
CAT03-062.figureLine: A single clay figure stands regaining its balance and footing, posture steadying and clarifying, conveying solid ground returning after confusion.
CAT03-062.sceneLine: A swirling, distorting soft plum-accent haze settles and clears around the figure into a calm, honest pale plum light, suggesting clarity and steadiness returning after manipulation.

CAT03-063.headline: Codependency
CAT03-063.subtitle: When helping others becomes self-neglect
CAT03-063.figureLine: A single clay figure that had been leaning its whole weight onto another form gently rights itself to stand on its own, posture rediscovering balance, conveying selfhood quietly restored.
CAT03-063.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent form once pooled entirely toward another figure draws back to rest evenly around the single figure in steady pale plum, suggesting care rebalanced toward the self.
